items.title: Neural net training on image data
items.scenario: A CNN trains for 100 epochs. Train accuracy reaches 99% by epoch 40, but validation accuracy peaks at 91% in epoch 22 and falls to 87% by epoch 40.
items.explanation: Early stopping with patience=5 on val/accuracy would halt around epoch 27 and restore weights from epoch 22 — avoiding the overfit tail where train keeps improving but validation degrades.

items.title: XGBoost on tabular fraud data
items.scenario: An engineer sets early_stopping_rounds=20 on val/logloss. Validation loss improves for rounds 1–85, plateaus, then rises after round 110.
items.explanation: Training stops at round 105 (20 rounds after the best at round 85). Predictions use best_iteration=85, not the full 105 trees — fewer trees with better generalization.

items.title: Small validation set with noisy curves
items.scenario: A startup has 500 training rows and 80 validation rows. Validation F1 jumps ±0.05 between consecutive epochs.
items.explanation: A patience of 3 may stop prematurely on noise. The team increases patience to 15 and adds a min_delta of 0.01 so tiny fluctuations do not reset or exhaust patience incorrectly.

items.title: Regression model with val/rmse monitoring
items.scenario: A housing price model uses Keras EarlyStopping on val/rmse with restore_best_weights=True and patience=10.
items.explanation: Training halts when val/rmse fails to improve for 10 epochs. Keras reloads weights from the epoch with lowest val/rmse — the deployed model matches the best validation checkpoint, not epoch at stop.

items.title: Gradient boosting without restore — silent bug
items.scenario: A team trains LightGBM with early stopping but calls predict on the model after 200 rounds when best iteration was 142.
items.explanation: Without using best_iteration, late trees overfit training residuals. Validation AUC at round 142 was 0.84; at round 200 it dropped to 0.79 — restore best weights fixes deployment.

items.title: Monitoring curves in an experiment tracker
items.scenario: An ML engineer logs train/loss and val/loss each epoch to W&B. At epoch 18, val/loss diverges upward while train/loss keeps falling.
items.explanation: The validation curve makes overfitting visible before manual inspection. Early stopping configured on val/loss with patience=5 automates the stop decision the engineer would make from the chart.
